comment_text.sn38.14:1.4: Also at DN 33:1.10.57 and SN 45.165:1.3; see note there.

root_text.sn38.14:1.6: “Atthi panāvuso maggo atthi paṭipadā, etāsaṁ dukkhatānaṁ pariññāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.14:1.7: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etāsaṁ dukkhatānaṁ pariññāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.14:1.8: “Katamo panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā, etāsaṁ dukkhatānaṁ pariññāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.14:1.9: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo, etāsaṁ dukkhatānaṁ pariññāya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.14:1.11: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā, etāsaṁ dukkhatānaṁ pariññāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.14:1.12: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā, etāsaṁ dukkhatānaṁ pariññāya.

translation_text.sn38.14:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘suffering’.
translation_text.sn38.14:1.3: “Reverend, there are these three forms of suffering.
translation_text.sn38.14:1.4: The suffering inherent in painful feeling; the suffering inherent in conditions; and the suffering inherent in perishing.
translation_text.sn38.14:1.5: These are the three forms of suffering.”
translation_text.sn38.14:1.6: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for completely understanding these forms of suffering?”
